source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Na Kika Platform Pollution Event

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 474

event.top_event: Pollution event - discharge of oil into Gulf of America waters
actions_taken: Reverted to original emulsion breaker formula
Submitted incident report to BSEE eWell system
BSEE investigation team deployed to platform
event.summary: On 08 February 2025, BP personnel at Na Kika platform began injecting a trial emulsion breaker chemical into the produced water treatment system. The new chemical caused frothing and foaming within production vessels, leading to faulty level readings and liquid carryover. This resulted in approximately 89 barrels of oil and grease discharging from the produced water overboard system into the Gulf of America from 08-15 February 2025. The discharge went undetected for 7 days due to the discharge point being located 80 feet below the water surface. Once BP identified the chemical trial as the cause, they discontinued the trial and returned to the original formula, successfully ending the pollution event.
recommendations: BSEE Production Operations Support Section should reach out to industry to require additional safeguards to detect system upsets more quickly
Develop alternative methods to detect pollution events besides visual observation from the platform, given the discharge point location 80 feet below water surface
Improve monitoring systems to enable faster identification of unfavorable chemical trial results

hazards.name: Inadequate monitoring of chemical trial effects

threats.name: Emulsion breaker chemical causes frothing and foaming in production vessels
Frothing leads to faulty level readings in separator vessels
Faulty level readings cause liquid carryover from vessels
Delayed detection of pollution event due to subsurface discharge point location

consequences.name: Discharge of 89 barrels of oil into Gulf of America waters
Violation of NPDES daily maximum effluent limits (42 mg/l)
Environmental pollution and water quality degradation

controls.name: Emulsion breaker chemical trial approval process
controls.barrier_role: Management approval gate for chemical trials

supporting_text: Although the chemical trial was approved by BSEE, the trial chemical proved to be non-compatible in the produced water system

controls.name: Control room monitoring of produced water system parameters
controls.barrier_role: Real-time monitoring of separator pressures, levels and system trends
controls.lod_basis: Continuous monitoring during chemical trial period

supporting_text: The separator pressures, levels and upsets were documented, and the system trends were being recorded and analyzed throughout the chemical trial
Unfortunately, the oil discharge was not immediately realized

controls.name: Topside vessel physical monitoring by production operators
controls.barrier_role: Visual and physical inspection of production vessels during chemical trial

supporting_text: production operators physically monitored the topside vessels
BP personnel were not trained properly on what to look for during this chemical trial. They failed to identify issues with the chemical trial

controls.name: Visual observation of produced water discharge for sheening
controls.barrier_role: Detection of pollution event through visual observation from platform
controls.lod_basis: Standard practice for detecting overboard discharge anomalies

supporting_text: The produced water outlet is located 80 feet below the water surface. Strong currents, foaming within vessels caused by the trial chemical leading to topside vessel upsets made it difficult for control room operators to quickly identify and determine the unfavorable results

As the produced water outlet is located well below the water surface, operators should have other methods in place to detect pollution events, besides looking over the side of the platform for a sheen

controls.name: Hazard analysis for chemical trial
controls.barrier_role: Identification of potential adverse effects of new chemical on production system

supporting_text: No or inadequate hazard analysis: BP personnel were not trained properly on what to look for during this chemical trial

controls.name: Personnel training on chemical trial monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators understand what to monitor and identify during chemical trial

supporting_text: BP personnel were not trained properly on what to look for during this chemical trial. They failed to identify issues with the chemical trial, and the pollution event went unnoticed for 7 days

controls.name: Reversion to original emulsion breaker formula
controls.barrier_role: Corrective action to stop pollution event once chemical trial identified as cause
controls.lod_basis: Immediate corrective action taken by BP

supporting_text: Once BP realized that the chemical trial was causing the pollution, and reverted to the original EB formulation
BP began returning to the original EB chemical formula and was successful in ending the sheening

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
